name: Pine Apple Inn & Bakehouse's Burgundy Shallot
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and finely slice the shallots. Peel the garlic.
2. Heat a little olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the shallot slices and garlic, and sauté for 2 to 3 minutes until the shallots are lightly golden.
3. Deglaze the pan with balsamic vinegar, reducing it until most of the vinegar has evaporated. Add the wine and thyme, then bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer until the shallots have absorbed all the liquid.
4. Gradually add the veal stock while stirring. Return to a gentle simmer and skim off any scum that rises to the surface. Remove from heat and discard the garlic and thyme.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve with grilled beef or veal. For added richness, whisk in 1 or 2 teaspoons of unsalted butter. Makes about 2 cups sauce or serves 4 to 6 people.
This dish was published by Toronto Star Starweek, Mary McGrath's Chef's Showcase on March 12-19, 1994. The recipe was requested by Al Malinauskas of Thornhill, Ontario, who celebrated his and his wife's 25th wedding anniversary at the Pine Apple Inn & Bakehouse in Unionville, Ontario. Credit for the cooking goes to chef David Watt.
